stemHtml: The World Health Organization (WHO) is a United Nations agency established in 1948 and headquartered in Geneva. It aims to achieve the highest possible level of health for people worldwide, defining health as a state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being. Among its various studies, the WHO has examined alcoholism, identifying its signs and symptoms, the groups at risk, and the impacts on the lives of those addicted. Determine which of the following individuals, according to the WHO definition, is most likely to be an alcoholic.

options.a: A middle-aged man in a highly regarded job drinks greater amounts of a special brand of whiskey on weekends and holidays. He will drive great distances at any time to buy a bottle of whiskey and revert to alternatives when his brand is not available. His tolerance towards alcohol has increased over the years, along with the average amount he drinks. He laughs off concerns voiced about health effects of his alcohol consumption.
options.b: A young woman has always been drinking moderate amounts of alcohol, preferably sparkling wine mixed with orange juice, on social occasions. She has just recently discovered that she has finally become pregnant, and is in pleasant anticipation of the time ahead. She decides to drink half a glass of champagne on a New Year’s Eve party, despite the warnings of two friends partying with her that alcohol could have adverse effects on her baby.
options.c: A teenager regularly drinks great amounts of alcohol in company of peers about twice a month, especially on bigger events, but also on occasions such a birthday parties. He has been hospitalised with alcohol intoxication previously, and his closer friends regularly keep an eye on his consumption once he has passed a certain threshold.
options.d: A widow woman drinks a glass of wine almost every evening, preferring quality dry red wine from Italy. She has made it a ritual to prepare her drink in one of the wine glasses that have been her wedding gift and raising her glass to a photograph of her deceased husband before drinking. When there is no wine left in her apartment, she reverts to eating great amounts of chocolate and buys a new bottle of wine on her next shopping tour. She justifies her consumption with the positive effect on the arteries.
